branding.headline: Dictation, Speech Recognition and Transcription Solutions for Australian Professionals
branding.subheadline: From traditional digital dictation hardware to modern cloud speech workflows, find the right solution for your practice, business or team. 25 years of Australian deployment experience.
branding.statsMessage: 25 years of Australian dictation and speech recognition expertise
branding.pathwaysHeading: Find the right solution for your workflow
branding.pathwaysSubheading: Three pathways for Australian professionals. Choose the approach that fits how you work.
branding.aboutHeading: Voice Recognition Australia - 25 years of dictation expertise
branding.aboutBody: Dictation Solutions Australia is part of the Voice Recognition Australia network - Australia's longest-serving speech recognition and dictation specialist. Our team has deployed Dragon, Philips and Olympus dictation systems across healthcare, legal, government and enterprise environments since the late 1990s. We help organisations choose the right solution, not just sell product.
branding.ctaHeading: Not sure which approach is right for you?
branding.ctaBody: Our team has been helping Australian professionals choose dictation and speech recognition solutions for 25 years. Tell us about your workflow and we will point you in the right direction.
ctaBullets: What you are trying to dictate and where
Whether you need hardware, software or both
Your industry and any compliance requirements
Number of users and deployment environment
branding.footerDisclaimer: Dictation Solutions Australia is part of the Voice Recognition Australia network. Dragon and related marks are the property of Nuance Communications (Microsoft). Philips and Olympus dictation products are trademarks of their respective owners. Voice Recognition Australia is an authorised Australian reseller. All prices AUD including GST where shown.

pathways.title: Dragon Speech Recognition
pathways.subtitle: Desktop and cloud software
pathways.description: Dragon Professional for Windows desktops, Dragon Medical One for healthcare, and Dragon Professional Anywhere for cloud-connected enterprise. Dictate faster than you type, automate repetitive workflows, and keep working offline.

pathways.title: Cloud Speech Solutions
pathways.subtitle: Browser-based AI transcription
pathways.description: Modern cloud speech recognition accessible from any device without local software installation. Supports 57 languages, tiered plans for individuals and organisations, and a free tier to get started.

pathways.title: Dictation Hardware
pathways.subtitle: Recorders, microphones and accessories
pathways.description: Philips and Olympus digital voice recorders, professional microphones, foot pedals, headsets and accessories for traditional and hybrid dictation workflows.

benefits.title: Hardware and software expertise
benefits.body: We cover the full dictation workflow - from Philips and Olympus voice recorders and foot pedals through to Dragon speech recognition software and cloud transcription platforms. One contact, complete solution.

benefits.title: Healthcare and legal focus
benefits.body: Most of our clients work in healthcare, legal or government. Our team understands the specific requirements of medical documentation, legal transcription, and secure enterprise dictation environments.

benefits.title: Modern and traditional workflows
benefits.body: Not everyone is ready to move to cloud AI speech tools. We support traditional dictation recorder workflows alongside modern Dragon and cloud speech solutions - whichever works for your team.

benefits.title: Australian support
benefits.body: Local deployment support from Voice Recognition Australia. Real people who understand your workflow, not a support ticket queue in another timezone.

proofPoints: 25 years of Australian dictation and speech recognition expertise
Dragon authorised reseller since product launch in Australia
Healthcare, legal and government deployment experience

faqs.q: What is the difference between Dragon and a dictation recorder?
faqs.a: Dragon software converts your speech to text in real time as you dictate into a computer or microphone. A digital dictation recorder captures your voice as an audio file, which is then transcribed later - either manually by a typist or via software. Many professionals use both: a recorder in the field, Dragon at the desk.

faqs.q: Do I need Dragon software or is cloud speech recognition enough?
faqs.a: It depends on your workflow. Dragon Professional works offline, has deep Windows application integration, and can automate complex tasks by voice. Cloud speech recognition is accessible from any browser, requires no local installation, and is often more cost-effective for occasional users. We can help you work out which approach suits your situation.

faqs.q: What dictation hardware do you recommend for healthcare?
faqs.a: For healthcare workflows, Philips SpeechMike and SpeechOne devices are widely used with Dragon Medical One. Olympus digital recorders work well for clinicians who prefer to dictate remotely and have audio transcribed. The right choice depends on whether you are dictating directly into an EMR or using a separate transcription workflow.

faqs.q: Is Dragon Medical One available in Australia?
faqs.a: Yes. Dragon Medical One is available through Voice Recognition Australia, an authorised Nuance/Microsoft reseller. It is a cloud-based subscription product designed specifically for healthcare documentation. Pricing is per user per year - contact Voice Recognition Australia for current Australian pricing.

faqs.q: What is a foot pedal used for in dictation?
faqs.a: A foot pedal lets a typist or transcriptionist control audio playback hands-free during transcription - play, pause, rewind and fast-forward using their feet while their hands remain on the keyboard. Philips and Olympus both make foot pedals designed for their respective dictation workflows.

faqs.q: Can Dragon work with Philips or Olympus voice recorders?
faqs.a: Dragon Professional can transcribe audio files from digital voice recorders, including Philips and Olympus devices, as long as the recording is single-speaker audio in a supported format (DS2, DSS, MP3, WAV, WMA). This is useful for professionals who prefer to dictate away from their desk and transcribe later.
